area_description: Question Answering with LLMs encompasses techniques and architectures for answering questions using large language models, spanning closed-book parametric approaches, retrieval-augmented open-domain methods, multi-hop reasoning, conversational interactions, long-form generation with attribution, and structured data querying.

categories.name: Closed-book QA
categories.description: Approaches where the LLM answers questions solely from its parametric knowledge without external retrieval, including methods for probing, measuring, and editing the knowledge stored in model parameters.

sub_topics.name: Parametric Knowledge
sub_topics.description: Leveraging knowledge encoded in LLM parameters during pre-training to answer questions without external sources.

sub_topics.name: Knowledge Probing
sub_topics.description: Methods for probing and measuring what factual knowledge LLMs have internalized, using cloze-style tests, prompting, and diagnostic probes.

sub_topics.name: Knowledge Editing for QA
sub_topics.description: Techniques for updating or correcting factual knowledge in LLMs to improve question answering accuracy without full retraining.

categories.name: Open-domain QA
categories.description: Methods that combine retrieval from external knowledge sources with LLM reading comprehension to answer questions from broad domains, including retrieval-augmented pipelines, passage reranking, and reader models.

sub_topics.name: Retrieval-augmented QA
sub_topics.description: Integrating document or passage retrieval with LLM-based answer generation, where the model conditions on retrieved evidence to produce answers.

sub_topics.name: Passage Reranking for QA
sub_topics.description: Using LLMs or cross-encoders to rerank retrieved passages for relevance before answer extraction, improving retrieval precision.

sub_topics.name: Reader Models
sub_topics.description: Models specialized in extracting or generating answers from retrieved passages, including extractive span selection and generative answer production.

categories.name: Multi-hop QA
categories.description: Approaches for answering questions that require reasoning over multiple pieces of evidence, including decomposition strategies, iterative retrieval, and graph-based reasoning methods.

sub_topics.name: Decomposition-based Multi-hop QA
sub_topics.description: Breaking complex multi-hop questions into simpler sub-questions that can be answered independently and composed into a final answer.

sub_topics.name: Iterative Retrieval
sub_topics.description: Performing multiple rounds of retrieval where each round is informed by previously retrieved evidence and intermediate reasoning.

sub_topics.name: Graph-based Reasoning
sub_topics.description: Using knowledge graphs or constructing reasoning graphs from evidence to connect entities and facts across multiple hops.

categories.name: Conversational QA
categories.description: Techniques for question answering in multi-turn dialogue settings, handling context-dependent follow-up questions, coreference resolution, and clarification strategies.

sub_topics.name: Dialogue-based QA
sub_topics.description: QA systems that maintain conversational context across multiple turns, resolving references and tracking topic shifts in dialogue.

sub_topics.name: Follow-up Questions
sub_topics.description: Handling context-dependent follow-up questions that build on previous answers, requiring coreference resolution and conversation history understanding.

sub_topics.name: Clarification
sub_topics.description: Systems that ask clarifying questions when queries are ambiguous or under-specified, and handle user clarifications to refine answers.

categories.name: Long-form QA
categories.description: Methods for generating detailed, paragraph-length or longer answers to complex questions, with emphasis on attribution, citation, and verification of generated content.

sub_topics.name: Long-form Answer Generation
sub_topics.description: Generating comprehensive, multi-sentence or multi-paragraph answers that fully address complex or open-ended questions.

sub_topics.name: Attribution and Citation
sub_topics.description: Methods for grounding generated answers in source documents by providing inline citations, attributions, or references to supporting evidence.

sub_topics.name: Answer Verification
sub_topics.description: Techniques for verifying the factual correctness of generated answers, including claim-level verification and entailment-based checking.

categories.name: Table and Structured QA
categories.description: Approaches for answering questions over structured data such as tables, databases, and knowledge bases, including table understanding, SQL generation, and structured data reasoning with LLMs.

sub_topics.name: Table QA
sub_topics.description: Question answering directly over tabular data, including cell lookup, aggregation, and reasoning over table contents using LLMs.

sub_topics.name: SQL Generation
sub_topics.description: Translating natural language questions into SQL queries to answer questions over relational databases, including text-to-SQL methods.

sub_topics.name: Structured Data Reasoning
sub_topics.description: Reasoning over structured data formats including knowledge bases, APIs, and semi-structured documents to answer questions.

theme.name: Factual Accuracy in QA
theme.description: Papers focused on improving and measuring the factual correctness of LLM-generated answers, including hallucination detection and faithfulness.

theme.name: Domain-specific QA
theme.description: Papers applying QA techniques to specialized domains such as medicine, law, science, and finance, addressing domain-specific challenges.

theme.name: Multilingual QA
theme.description: Papers addressing question answering across multiple languages, including cross-lingual transfer, multilingual retrieval, and low-resource language QA.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
